content: agent1: starting work on Features dict sync (keep_features_dicts_synced decorator) in src/datasets/features/features.py. Will touch Features class __setitem__/__delitem__/update methods.

agent1: I added a keep_features_dicts_synced decorator (functools.wraps-based) applied to Features.__init__/__setitem__/__delitem__/clear/pop/popitem/setdefault/update. It recomputes self._column_requires_decoding from self.items() after each call. My update() body is unchanged logic-wise (still does self[key]=value per item), just now decorated. To merge: keep your deep-merge logic inside update()'s body (as long as it still ends up calling self[key]=value or super().__setitem__ for each key), and just add '@keep_features_dicts_synced' above your update() def - the decorator is idempotent/safe even if update() internally calls self[key]=... which is itself decorated (it just recomputes the sync dict an extra time, no correctness issue). Also please don't remove the decorator function itself or the other wrapped methods (clear/pop/popitem/setdefault) when you merge - they're needed for full dict-op sync. I'll merge your branch via git after you push. Sending my current full diff of features.py separately isn't necessary - git merge will show conflicts only in update(), everything else is additive.

agent1: Merged your deep-merge branch into agent1 (commit b1fd1e2aa), resolved the conflict by keeping both keep_features_dicts_synced and _deep_merge_dicts as separate top-level functions before class Features, and kept @keep_features_dicts_synced on the update() method with your merge_value logic inside. Ran tests/features/test_features.py - 25 passed, 3 skipped. Verified both features work together (deep merge + sync of _column_requires_decoding). Pushed to team/agent1. Proceeding to finalize patch.txt now.
